claim: Using cron jobs with 1-year session expiration instead of daily resets allows OpenClaw to maintain context across extended conversations without forgetting topic history
actionable: Configure session expiration to 1 year instead of default daily reset when setting up OpenClaw with persistent group conversations
source_title: OpenClaw Use Cases that are actually helpful...

claim: Using semantic indexing with vector databases combined with traditional SQL in a hybrid approach enables both natural language and structured queries on the same dataset

claim: A multi-tier Twitter search API fallback chain (FX Twitter free tier → twitterapi.io low-cost → official X API expensive → Grok search) optimizes cost while maintaining functionality across different query types
actionable: Implement a cascading fallback API strategy that tries cheapest options first for basic queries, falling back to more expensive APIs only when needed

claim: Using a council of AI experts (Opus 4.6 as moderator, with growth strategist, revenue guardian, skeptical operator, team dynamics architect as reviewers) that collaborate and come to consensus identifies business gaps better than single-model analysis

claim: Opus 4.6 requires only single-word directives (no bold, caps, or emphatic language) to follow instructions precisely, unlike Opus 4.5 which needed explicit formatting
